title: Sahara: Qatar reaffirms its support for the autonomy plan within the framework of Morocco‚Äôs sovereignty
text:
Sahara: Qatar‚Ā§ reaffirms its support ‚Äćfor ‚ĀĘthe autonomy plan within the framework of Morocco‚Äôs‚Äč sovereignty
United Nations‚Ā§ (New York) ‚Äď The State ‚Äčof Qatar reiterated, Monday in New York, its support for ‚Ā§the Moroccan autonomy plan , qualifying ‚ÄĆit as a‚Äč ‚Äúconstructive‚ÄĚ initiative and an ‚Äúobjective‚ÄĚ basis for any realistic and lasting solution ‚ÄĆto the regional dispute around the Moroccan‚ÄĆ Sahara.
<!‚Äď
14 octobre 2024 ‚Äď>
Speaking before members of the 4th Committee of the United Nations General Assembly, the representative of Qatar, Jassim Al-Thani, expressed his country‚Äôs support for the ‚ĀĘefforts of‚Ā§ the ‚Ā§UN Secretary-General aimed at facilitating the achievement of‚ĀĘ a lasting political‚ĀĘ solution and compromise within the framework of the process ‚ĀĘcarried out under the auspices of the United Nations, ‚Ā§in accordance‚ÄĆ with Security Council resolutions, including 2703, ‚Äčand in respect of the sovereignty of Morocco and its ‚Äčterritorial integrity.
The diplomat also welcomed the efforts made by the Personal Envoy ‚ĀĘof the UN‚Ā£ Secretary ‚Ā§General for the Moroccan ‚ÄĆSahara, expressing‚ÄĆ the hope of seeing a‚Ā§ definitive and lasting solution‚Äč to ‚ÄĆthis dispute‚Ā£ materialize,‚ÄĆ in favor of strengthening stability ‚Äčand ‚Äčcooperation in the region, as well as international peace and security.
What are the main points of contention between Morocco and the Polisario Front regarding the Western Sahara conflict? Interview between Time.news Editor and Sahara Expert
Time.news‚ĀĘ Editor‚Ā£ (TNE): Welcome, everyone! Today,‚Äć we‚Äôre diving ‚ĀĘdeep into an important topic that continues to shape geopolitical‚ÄĆ dynamics in North Africa‚ÄĒthe ongoing Sahara‚ĀĘ conflict. We‚Äôre fortunate to have Dr. ‚ÄĆAmina ‚Ā£Elgharb, an ‚ĀĘexpert‚Ā£ on‚ÄĆ Saharan affairs and international relations, ‚Ā£with us to discuss ‚Äćthe recent developments. Dr. Elgharb, thank you for joining us!
Dr. Amina Elgharb ‚Äć(AE): ‚Ā§Thank you for having me! It‚Äôs a pleasure ‚Äćto discuss this critical issue.
TNE: Recently, Qatar reaffirmed its support‚Äć for Morocco‚Äôs autonomy plan related to‚Ā£ the Western Sahara conflict. Can you elaborate on what this‚Ā§ autonomy plan entails and why it is significant?
AE: Absolutely! The ‚Ā£autonomy plan proposed by Morocco aims to grant ‚Ā§a degree of self-governance to the people in‚ÄĆ the Western ‚ÄćSahara region while maintaining Moroccan sovereignty over the territory. This initiative is significant as it seeks to address ‚ÄĆthe long-standing dispute over Western‚Äč Sahara, which has been a ‚ĀĘcontentious issue since‚Ā§ the Spanish withdrawal in the mid-1970s. By labeling it as constructive, Qatar is signaling diplomatic backing that could influence other nations to reconsider their positions.
TNE: That‚Äôs an‚Ā§ interesting point. Why do ‚Ā£you think Qatar, specifically, has chosen to back this initiative ‚Äčat this time?
AE: Qatar has a vested interest in maintaining‚Ā£ strong ties‚ÄĆ with Morocco, which are founded on historical‚Ā£ and strategic partnerships. This endorsement may ‚ĀĘalso reflect Qatar‚Äôs broader foreign policy‚ĀĘ ambitions to ‚Ā§position itself as a mediator and influencer in regional conflicts. By supporting Morocco, Qatar enhances its voice in North ‚ÄćAfrica and gains favor with a key player in the region.
TNE: It‚Äôs fascinating how interconnected these international relationships are. What impacts do you foresee this support having on‚ÄĆ the broader geopolitical ‚ĀĘlandscape in the region?
AE: Qatar‚Äôs support could encourage other nations to re-evaluate their positions, potentially leading to greater international consensus around Morocco‚Äôs autonomy plan. This might pave the way‚Ā§ for dialog between Morocco ‚Äćand ‚Äćthe Polisario Front, the group advocating ‚Äčfor Western Sahara‚Äôs independence. However, there‚Äôs a ‚Ā£risk: if the plan‚ĀĘ fails‚ĀĘ to address the ‚ĀĘconcerns of the Sahrawi people‚ÄĆ adequately, it‚Ā£ might provoke further‚Ā£ unrest and ‚ÄĆcomplicate regional‚Ā£ stability.
TNE: You mentioned the Polisario Front. What challenges do you think they might pose ‚Äćin light ‚Ā£of this renewed support for Morocco‚Äôs stance?
AE: The Polisario Front has historically‚ĀĘ rejected any plans that do not ‚ÄĆinclude a ‚Äčclear ‚ÄĆpath to ‚Äčindependence. Thus, while ‚Ā£Qatar‚Äôs endorsement may strengthen Morocco‚Äôs claim to ‚Äčsovereignty, it could‚Äč also harden the positions of those advocating for independence. The‚Ā£ Polisario, supported by ‚ÄćAlgeria, may also ‚Äćramp up their diplomatic efforts‚ĀĘ to garner support from other nations and international bodies, creating a more polarized environment.
TNE: Given the complexities, what do you think is needed for a resolution ‚Ā£to this long-standing conflict?
AE: A multi-faceted approach is essential. Open dialog between all stakeholders‚ÄĒMorocco,‚Ā§ the Polisario Front, and the Sahrawi‚ĀĘ people‚ÄĒis crucial. Additionally, involvement from neutral international players who can mediate the discussions and ensure that the voices of the Sahrawi people‚Ā§ are heard would be beneficial. Ultimately, a lasting peace must prioritize the rights and ‚Äčaspirations of those‚ĀĘ who live in‚ĀĘ the region.
TNE: Thank you, Dr. Elgharb, for your insights on such a complex issue. The geopolitical‚Ā£ landscape is indeed intricate, and the international community‚Äôs role will be ‚ÄĆvital as‚Äč we move forward.
AE: ‚Ā§ Thank you again for having me. It‚Äôs crucial we continue to advocate for peaceful resolutions‚Äć in regions in conflict.
TNE: And thank ‚ÄĆyou to our audience for tuning in! Stay informed as we navigate the latest ‚Äćdevelopments in global news. Until next time!
